So I like to say, yeah, like part of our job is always to kind of like show continuous improvement.
So we do have those short-term wins, whether they're like, you

I do monthly and weekly reporting, so increases in organic search traffic or increases in keyword rankings, I think those are great, but I think it's always good to keep in mind that the bigger picture, the goal of kind of where we're at and keeping in mind our competition.

So that's why I like organic share of voice metric, looking if we're authoritative sources for rich results, and there's AI searches, because that's pretty much the way everything is trending, it's not going to be like people searching, it's going to be personalized searches, so making sure our intent is really focused is pretty much the biggest broad initiative that you should be focused on.

And going to also, like, offensive and defensive.
Like a mindset.
So from like an offensive standpoint, like you want to be increasing your topical authority.
So like deploying schema, things like improving your topical authority, all like kind of content optimization efforts.
And then defensive SEO would be like making sure the user experience is, you know, up to par.
We don't want to have a poor user experience.
So make sure there's no like 404, you know, broken like internal, external links.
We want to make sure everything's like indexed, all those technical things.
